sims.title: The BrightPath Outage: Students Locked Out
sims.summary: A learning platform goes dark during midterm week. Students can't reach the site, but the server says everything is fine.

sims.title: The Meridian Health Data Leak
sims.summary: A healthcare company's private documents are suddenly accessible to anyone on the internet. The compliance team is panicking.

sims.title: The CropSync Harvest Crisis: Database Full
sims.summary: Harvest season hits and the farm data platform freezes. New crop readings won't save, but nobody changed the code.

sims.title: PacketForge Lambda Lockout: The Missing Permission
sims.summary: A threat intelligence pipeline that ran for months starts failing overnight. The code hasn't changed, but something else did.

sims.title: UrbanFleet Rush Hour Meltdown: The 502 Storm
sims.summary: A ride-sharing app crashes at rush hour with a wall of 502 errors. The servers are running, but nobody can reach them.

sims.title: A Function in the Wrong Room
sims.summary: A booking API returns nothing but errors after a routine deployment. The function exists, the code is correct, yet every call fails.

sims.title: Four Million Records, One by One
sims.summary: A product catalog search gets slower every week. Four million records, and the team swears the queries used to be fast.

sims.title: The Upload That Worked Everywhere Else
sims.summary: File uploads work perfectly from Postman and curl, but the browser keeps refusing. The presigned URLs are valid.

sims.title: Someone Else's Keys
sims.summary: An application starts using credentials that belong to someone who left the company months ago. Nobody changed anything.

sims.title: The Stack That Wouldn't Move
sims.summary: A deployment stack won't update, won't roll back, and won't delete. Something vanished over the weekend.

sims.title: Nine Hundred Dollars for Nothing
sims.summary: The AWS bill jumps by nine hundred dollars a day. The workload hasn't changed, but something is quietly burning money.

sims.title: The Sale That Started Too Fast
sims.summary: A retailer's flash sale crashes the site within sixty seconds of launch. The scaling rules are set, but they can't keep up.

sims.title: The Targets That Disappeared
sims.summary: Every deployment causes a brief outage. Old instances vanish before new ones are ready, and users hit dead ends.

sims.title: The Queue Nobody Watched
sims.summary: Customers are getting charged twice for the same order. The code processes each message once, or so the team believes.

sims.title: Sixteen Seconds of Nothing
sims.summary: The backup site exists and works, but when the primary goes down, traffic goes nowhere. Failover was supposed to be automatic.

sims.title: A Notification for No One
sims.summary: Alarms fire, but nobody gets notified. Email, Slack, and SMS are all configured, yet every channel is silent.

sims.title: The Cache That Forgot Everything
sims.summary: Response times spike from milliseconds to eight seconds. The cache is running, the database is running, but something snapped.

sims.title: The Agent That Could Not Act
sims.summary: An AI agent can reason through every step of a plan, but when it tries to act, nothing happens. The Lambda function never fires.

sims.title: One Thousand and Twenty-Four
sims.summary: A knowledge base search returns confident but completely wrong answers. The documents are correct, the model is fine.

sims.title: Intermittent by Design
sims.summary: Roughly one in three AI requests fails for no apparent reason. Retrying the same request sometimes works, sometimes doesn't.

sims.title: Ten Dollars a Minute
sims.summary: An AI assistant that cost eight hundred dollars a week suddenly costs twelve thousand. Nothing in the code changed.

sims.title: The Guardrail and the Doctor
sims.summary: A patient triage assistant blocks nearly half of legitimate medical questions. The guardrail is doing its job too well.

sims.title: The Endpoint That Stopped Thinking
sims.summary: A machine learning endpoint freezes every morning under rush-hour load. The scaling policy exists but never fires.

sims.title: The Agent That Would Not Stop
sims.summary: An AI research assistant keeps thinking long after the user left. The bill climbs by ten dollars a minute and there is no visible way to kill it.

sims.title: The Agent With the Wrong Keys
sims.summary: A new AI data analyst keeps answering questions about payroll data nobody authorized it to see. Its execution role was generated by a wizard.

sims.title: The Thirteen-Hour Recreation
sims.summary: The DevOps agent was asked to fix staging drift. Four minutes later it deletes the production stack. The approval gate that should have caught it never fired.

sims.title: The Filter That Lost the Answer
sims.summary: A legal research assistant returns correct cases when asked broadly. Filter by jurisdiction and it starts inventing citations that sound right. The embeddings look fine.

sims.title: Yesterday's Token, Today's Traffic
sims.summary: A Java payments Lambda starts failing every morning at six. The code has not changed. The only thing that changed is the cold start got faster.

sims.title: Five Minutes Is Not Enough
sims.summary: A support agent's catalog-sync tool times out at exactly five minutes every time. The agent silently retries, and customers get charged twice.

sims.title: The Door That Read the Wrong Name
sims.summary: An internal admin portal denies every employee at once. The zero-trust policy compiles, evaluates, and turns everyone away.

sims.title: Every Order, Again
sims.summary: Consumers are processing the same orders over and over. The Kafka cluster is fine, the code did not change, but offsets never stick.

sims.title: The Pipe That Said No to Everything
sims.summary: A fulfillment pipeline stops working the moment it is migrated to EventBridge Pipes. SQS has messages, Lambda is idle, nothing errors.

sims.title: The Service That Went Quiet
sims.summary: Attaching a VPC connector fixes database access and breaks everything else. Stripe calls time out, Slack is silent, nobody expected this.

sims.title: The Key the Cache Would Not Drop
sims.summary: A nightly cleanup job dies the first time it runs on the new durable cache. The cache is up, connection is fine, the command is refused.

sims.title: The Partner Who Saw Too Much
sims.summary: An external supplier's SFTP account was only supposed to see its own folder. CloudTrail says it read every customer's folder last night.

sims.title: The Lock You Wrote Yourself
sims.summary: Admin self-locks out of S3 bucket by writing a Deny policy on aws:SourceVpce that blocks non-VPC traffic, including the AWS Console

sims.title: The Proxy That Stopped Sharing
sims.summary: For 8 months, RDS Proxy multiplexed 400 Lambda sessions into 20 database connections. Monday afternoon every Lambda times out with connection-slots exhausted. No deployment happened.

sims.title: Thirty Seconds of Silence
sims.summary: A niche B2B reporting tool migrated to Aurora Serverless v2 with min_capacity=0. Every Monday morning the first customer login hangs for 30 seconds and API Gateway returns 504. The database shows no errors.

sims.title: The Route That Was Never There
sims.summary: A static blackhole route in the TGW route table has silently dropped payroll VPC east-west traffic for six months. Discovered via Reachability Analyzer during new-VPC routing setup.

sims.title: The Events Nobody Sent
sims.summary: A new product account's EventBridge rule fires and Invocations climb, but the central audit Lambda is never invoked. FailedInvocations is zero. No DLQ. No errors.
